title: Evening By Qianmen
subtitle: Qianmen / Dashilan / Sanlihe
blurb: Walk the southern old center, choose dinner by the queue, and finish by the water.
mood: Signs, dense streets, and a flexible dinner

microcopy.line: Walk the streets and compare queues before choosing dinner.

content.intro:
Start at Qianmen, walk Dashilan and Xianyukou, and choose dinner only after one loop. It is easy to lose the evening in a random queue here: a famous sign does not guarantee good seating today.

Keep Lao She and Sanlihe as choices after food. If program and energy line up, go for a tea performance; if not, move toward water or straight to the metro.
content.routeLogic:
At Qianmen, look at the gate, broad street, and side entrances.
In Dashilan, pass dense spots without stopping at every window.
On Xianyukou, read smell, queue movement, and seating, but do not eat too much before dinner.
Add Lao She only after checking the program.
Use Sanlihe as a short water-side exit before metro.
content.bestFor:
an evening after a light day or museum
a first dinner without a long transfer
travelers who want old signs, streets, and choices on the ground
content.notFor:
travelers who want a quiet restaurant without crowds
a full axis day without a rest
when you need to reach a show without checking the schedule
content.beforeYouGo:
Arrive before peak dinner so you are not choosing in the densest queue.
For old brands, check the exact address and current seating.
Check the Lao She program on the day through an official channel.
content.timing: Start 1.5-2 hours before dinner. Walk first, then sit down; add the teahouse or Sanlihe only after food.
content.fallbackPlan: If queues are not moving, turn into nearby streets or switch from duck to noodles, snacks, and tea. If rain starts, keep Qianmen, dinner, and a taxi back.

stopList.name: Qianmen
stopList.note: Look around before turning into Dashilan

content.context: Qianmen gives the evening a broad start: the gate behind you, the long street ahead, and side entrances into denser Dashilan. Do not spend your whole appetite on the first snacks.
content.lookFor:
View toward Zhengyangmen
Old shop signs
Side entrances into Dashilan
Where the flow gets denser
content.practical:
Evenings are busier.
Compare menus before sitting down.
Do not buy too much food before the main meal.
content.nextMove: Turn into Dashilan, where the streets narrow and get louder.

stopList.name: Dashilan
stopList.note: Notice the signs but skip weak stops

content.context: In Dashilan, stay on the main street only while it gives you new details: old lettering, narrow facades, and queues at known places. If windows repeat or the crowd stops, turn into a side street.
content.lookFor:
Old lettering on signs
Narrow facades
Queues at popular places
Passages toward Xianyukou
content.practical:
A queue does not always mean the best choice.
Check the exact address, not only the brand name.
Side streets are easier for a short breather.
content.nextMove: Move toward Xianyukou and decide what to save for dinner.

stopList.name: Xianyukou
stopList.note: Taste lightly and watch queue movement

content.context: Xianyukou works as a short food scout. You can see where food is fresh, what comes out fast, and where the queue keeps moving. Do not turn the street into a full dinner if you still want duck or a show.
content.lookFor:
Open kitchens
Fast-moving queues
Small portions
Exits to quieter streets
content.practical:
Take small portions.
Watch what people ahead of you order.
If the queue does not move, change places without regret.
content.nextMove: If you want a performance or tea, walk to Lao She; otherwise choose dinner nearby and then move toward water.

stopList.name: Lao She Teahouse
stopList.note: Go only if program and energy line up

content.context: Treat Lao She as a deliberate evening choice, not an automatic stop after food. After a heavy dinner, a show can become one obligation too many, so first check start time, seats, and length.
content.lookFor:
Current program
Start time
Available seats
How much energy is left after dinner
content.practical:
Check the schedule on the day.
Do not place the show tight against a late restaurant booking.
If the program does not fit, skip it calmly.
content.nextMove: After the performance, or instead of it, walk to Sanlihe or finish near the metro.
content.sourceNote: Programs, sales channels, and show times change; check the current schedule.

stopList.name: Sanlihe Park
stopList.note: A short water-side exit after food

content.context: Sanlihe is a softer exit from commercial noise: water, small bridges, and paths let you walk after dinner without more shop windows. If rain or fatigue is already heavy, replace the park with a taxi.
content.lookFor:
Canals and small bridges
Evening lighting
Quieter passages
Clear exits toward metro
content.practical:
Paths can be slippery after rain.
Late in the evening, stay near a clear exit.
If you are tired, call DiDi back to the hotel.
content.nextMove: Head to the nearest metro or return to the hotel by car.
